prompt:
We throttle bulk exports with a channel-as-semaphore and it starves large jobs: a job needing 2 slots waits forever while 1-slot jobs keep barging in. I need a real weighted semaphore with FIFO fairness. Package semaphore, root of module go-fair-semaphore, stdlib only (we can't vendor x/sync into this component, and I want Waiters() introspection anyway).

API:
- New(capacity int64) (*Semaphore, error), capacity >= 1
- Acquire(ctx context.Context, n int64) error — blocks until n tokens are granted or ctx is done. If ctx is already canceled, return its error without taking anything. n > capacity errors immediately (it can never succeed).
- TryAcquire(n int64) bool — non-blocking, and it must NOT barge: if anyone is queued, TryAcquire fails even when enough tokens are technically free. Queue-jumping is the exact starvation bug this replaces.
- Release(n int64) — returns tokens and wakes queued waiters. Releasing more than is currently held is a caller bug: panic.
- Waiters() int — how many goroutines are queued (we chart this).

Fairness rules: waiters are served strictly in arrival order. If the head of the queue needs more tokens than are free, everybody behind it waits — even waiters whose request would fit. A waiter that gives up (ctx canceled) comes out of the queue cleanly: it must not hold a phantom slot, and waiters behind it must still get served on the next Release.

semaphore_test.go is the acceptance contract (don't edit it); it hammers the thing from 16 goroutines under -race, so no cleverness with unsynchronized state. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass.
